taxonomy: Macrotomia guttata is classified as a species of beetle within the family Cerambycidae. This family is commonly known as longhorned beetles, characterized by their distinctive long antennae and elongated bodies. Macrotomia guttata is further classified within the subfamily Lamiinae, which includes several other longhorned beetle species. The classification of Macrotomia guttata is based on its physical characteristics, such as the shape and size of its body, as well as its genetic makeup. By understanding the classification of Macrotomia guttata, scientists can better comprehend its evolutionary history and relationships with other longhorned beetles.
